feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day

entry.title: Fery reaches third round to keep British hopes alive
entry.content: Arthur Fery keeps British hopes alive at Wimbledon as he fights back from a set down to reach the third round, but Katie Swan is out.

entry.title: Dusty May names Cooper Flagg as top reason for joining Mavericks
entry.content: Cooper Flagg was the main reason for why Dusty May left the Michigan Wolverines and joined the Dallas Mavericks.

entry.title: Barcelona Puts World Cup Star Up For Sale And Has Interested Parties, Reports SPORT
entry.content: FC Barcelona has effectively put Jules Kounde up for sale and has reduced his price tag amid interest from Chelsea and Liverpool in the Premier League.

entry.title: Nate Bittle's 2026 NBA Summer League schedule with Toronto Raptors
entry.content: Check out when you can catch former Oregon Ducks center Nate Bittle with the Toronto Raptors during the 2026 NBA Summer League in Las Vegas.

entry.title: Mavericks found an unexpected way to flip Anthony Davis into Santi Aldama
entry.content: The Mavericks used Anthony Davis trade assets to acquire Santi Aldama from the Grizzlies in a surprising offseason move.

entry.title: Braves recall Jim Jarvis, DFA Rowdy Tellez
entry.content: The Braves shuffle their infield and shunt Tellez off the roster

entry.title: PGA Tour’s Biggest Events Deliver Ratings Gains Ahead of TV Talks
entry.content: The $20 million events are a model for the new Championship Series.

entry.title: Gulls mourn death of 'Great Escape' striker Dobson
entry.content: Paul Dobson, the Torquay United striker whose goal kept the club in the Football League in 1987, dies aged 63.

entry.title: I Make $80,000 a Year and Dave Ramsey Told Me This Is Why I’m Staying Broke
entry.content: I Make $80,000 a Year and Dave Ramsey Told Me This Is Why I’m Staying Broke

entry.title: Tesla Deliveries Soar To 480,126 In Q2; EV Giant's Stock Eyes Buy Point (Live Coverage)
entry.content: Tesla Deliveries Soar To 480,126 In Q2; EV Giant's Stock Eyes Buy Point (Live Coverage)

entry.title: Forget SpaceX: Investors Say This Is The Next Breakout AI Stock in 2026
entry.content: Forget SpaceX: Investors Say This Is The Next Breakout AI Stock in 2026

entry.title: Retail Investors Say This Is The Next SanDisk Stock Everyone’s Ignoring
entry.content: Retail Investors Say This Is The Next SanDisk Stock Everyone’s Ignoring

entry.title: HDMI 2.1 vs USB-C vs DisplayPort: Which connection is better for your monitor?
entry.content: HDMI excels for media consumption, while PC gamers prefer DisplayPort.

entry.title: Donald Trump bought a bunch of tech stock the same day he announced his AI Action Plan
entry.content: Financial disclosures show how much Trump loves tech stocks.

entry.title: Threads' ubiquitous Mr Beast spam is part of a massive crypto scam network
entry.content: Fake Mr Beast "promotions" are ubiquitous on Threads - they're also part of massive crypto scam network.

entry.title: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, far below forecasts
entry.content: June's payroll gains were much lower than the 100,000 new hires that economists had predicted.

entry.title: Breaking down U.S. win against Bosnia and Herzegovina, controversial red card and next match
entry.content: Nigel Reo-Coker, an analyst for CBS Sports Golazo, joins "CBS Mornings" to break down the United States' big win against Bosnia and Herzegovina in the World Cup, its chances against Belgium and more.

entry.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
entry.content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

entry.title: What we know about Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
entry.content: Preparations are underway at Madison Square Garden in New York City for what could be Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding. But not all of Swift's fans are convinced the big day will be held at MSG. Jo Ling Kent reports on what we know about the superstar wedding.

entry.title: Russia unleashes major attack in Ukraine, kills at least 17
entry.content: Russia launched a deadly attack in Kyiv, Ukraine, following intense warnings that the ratcheted violence was imminent. CBS News' Aidan Stretch reports.

entry.title: Huge Russian strike on Ukraine's capital kills at least 17, officials say
entry.content: Russia has launched its large-scale attack on Ukraine's capital to date, killing at least 17 people and wounding scores more, authorities say.

entry.title: At least 17 killed, dozens injured in major Russian attack on Kyiv
entry.content: At least 17 people were killed and 90 injured in a Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv, officials say. Aidan Stretch has the latest.

entry.title: Dads of Camp Mystic victims push for safety changes nearly 1 year after deadly floods
entry.content: Nearly one year ago, flash flooding in Texas killed more than 130 people, including 27 campers and counselors at Camp Mystic. Jason Allen spoke with two fathers of Camp Mystic victims who are pushing for change.

entry.title: Rivian raises EV sales forecast as Q2 production ramps up
entry.content: The company now expects to ship a few thousand more vehicles by the end of 2026 than it previously expected, after launching its R2 SUV last month.

entry.title: What Germany’s Economic Overhaul Means for Its Economy
entry.content: The measures are a political gamble for a government that has seen its ratings plummet in the past year.

entry.title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
entry.content: Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.

entry.title: Beijing Says Pilot Who Crashed Into Skyscraper Had Suicidal Thoughts
entry.content: Authorities pointed to personal, not political, motivations after the 66-year-old flew his light aircraft into the Chinese capital’s tallest building.

entry.title: U.S.-Iran talks pause for ayatollah's funeral after claims of progress
entry.content: Negotiations between Iran and the U.S. will pause for a week for the funeral of Iran's slain supreme leader, but Trump, and mediators, say there's been progress.

entry.title: 2 Empire State Building climbers awaiting arraignment after apparently getting engaged at the top
entry.content: Two people climbed to the top of New York City's Empire State Building, unfurled a banner, and then apparently got engaged Wednesday afternoon.

entry.title: Extended interview: Gen Zers on COVID, generational stereotypes, AI and more
entry.content: In the last installment of our USA-Z series, Vladimir Duthiers speaks with seven Gen Zers about their generation being next to lead the nation, growing up during the pandemic, stereotypes they see about themselves, the role of AI in their world as they join the workforce and more.

entry.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
entry.content: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

entry.title: Boy driving truck crashes into group of monks, killing 8 and injuring 14
entry.content: Police said the boy had taken his parents' pickup truck without permission before losing control of the vehicle and crashing into the monks.

entry.title: U.S. designates "Chone Killers" gang a terrorist organization
entry.content: The gang "has committed numerous attacks targeting civilians, law enforcement officers, and government officials," Secretary of State Marco Rubio said.

entry.title: FACT FOCUS: Iran claims a foreign ship got stuck in Strait of Hormuz. But it is tied to Tehran
entry.content: FACT FOCUS: Iran claims a foreign ship got stuck in Strait of Hormuz. But it is tied to Tehran

entry.title: Massive Russian Strikes Pound Ukraine’s Capital, Killing at Least 17
entry.content: Kyiv was hit with ballistic and cruise missiles as well as waves of drones over several hours.

entry.title: How dialects reveal America's history and hint at what's next
entry.content: Across the United States, the way you speak is filled with cultural authenticity and central to identity.

entry.title: NBCUniversal spin marks new era of Hollywood moguls
entry.content: A new crop of leaders has emerged, reflecting Silicon Valley's vast influence.

entry.title: Toni Morrison wrote the real history of America. We're just finally reading it
entry.content: An Ohio statewide remix of Toni Morrison's oeuvre connects the ghosts of 'Beloved' to the neighborhoods of 'Sula' to reveal the real narratives of the American experiment.

entry.title: X is making a fresh push for live video with new creator payouts
entry.content: X has launched a new live streaming command center and additional creator payouts.

entry.title: Athletics lose Brent Rooker for season with knee injury
entry.content: One of Oakland's top offensive threats remains scheduled to undergo left knee surgery.

entry.title: Folarin Balogun red card, explained: What controversial call means for USMNT star’s availability for Round of 16
entry.content: With the USMNT leading Bosnia and Herzegovina 1-0 on a first-half goal from Folarin Balogun, the AS Monaco striker was given a straight red card that reduced Mauricio Pochettino's side to 10 men for the rest of the match.

entry.title: Can FIFA rescind a red card after it's given? Explaining appeal rules regarding Folarin Balogun suspension
entry.content: The United States have advanced to the Round of 16 at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, but they will have to prepare for their upcoming match against Belgium as if they will be without one of their best players.

entry.title: Celtics linked to blockbuster trade for Lakers' $112 former star target after Jaylen Brown deal
entry.content: The Boston Celtics might consider making another roster move after trading their star guard.

entry.title: Tim MacMahon reveals that front offices around the NBA did not like Jaylen Brown's value
entry.content: The NBA world was shocked when Jaylen Brown got traded to the 76ers, but it might not have been a surprise to front offices around the league.

entry.title: Who will USA play next? Here is USMNT's 2026 World Cup bracket opponent for Round of 16
entry.content: Here's a breakdown of the United States' full bracket in the World Cup.

entry.title: USMNT World Cup Round of 16 history: Revisiting last time USA won a knockout match in men's soccer
entry.content: Here's a glance at the years past to the last time the USMNT won a match in the World Cup knockout stage.

entry.title: Former NBA champion believes Paul George will be a great fit alongside Jayson Tatum
entry.content: Despite all the backlash the Celtics are getting, it seems some people believe that Paul George could be a difference-maker for Boston.

entry.title: CNN journalist jumps ship to MS NOW over fears of new leadership after merger: report
entry.content: CNN journalist jumps ship to MS NOW over fears of new leadership after merger: report

entry.title: Khamenei body in cold storage as feared Basij mobilizes ahead of historic Iran funeral
entry.content: Khamenei body in cold storage as feared Basij mobilizes ahead of historic Iran funeral

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: In 10 minutes, a hurricane releases more energy than all of the world's nuclear weapons combined.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
Every exit is an entrance to new experiences.
Lucky Numbers: 37, 26, 40, 94, 93, 88

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: Take a picture of business cards people give to you, just in case you lose it.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Carol Burnett
entry.content: “No one ever said life was fair. Just Eventful.” — Carol Burnett

entry.title: Recall of popular potato chips classified under FDA's highest risk level
entry.content: Recall of popular potato chips classified under FDA's highest risk level

entry.title: USA vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina live score, result, highlights from USMNT's 2026 World Cup knockout game
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and highlights for USA vs. Bosnia and Herzegovina in the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Follow for complete results from the Round of 32 match in San Francisco.

entry.title: Jaylen Brown trade details: Sixers acquire former Finals MVP in deal with Celtics
entry.content: The Celtics didn't land Giannis Antetokounmpo, but they are breaking up their core by dealing Jaylen Brown. Here are the details.

entry.title: Celtics projected lineup with Paul George: How former 76er fits in with Jayson Tatum, others in wake of Jaylen Brown trade
entry.content: Here's how the Celtics could line up to begin next year.

entry.title: NHL free agency tracker 2026: Live updates on news, rumors, signings and trades
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking the latest NHL free agency news and rumors.

entry.title: Preseason doubts vanish as White Sox net a perfect midpoint grade
entry.content: The White Sox look like the real deal.

entry.title: Why Belgium was awarded late, game-winning penalty in comeback win over Senegal at the World Cup
entry.content: Belgium have struggled throughout much of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and they looked destined for a disappointing Round of 32 exit before grabbing victory from the jaws of defeat.

entry.title: Crypto Bets and Real Estate Deals: Inside Trump’s $2 Billion Year
entry.content: The president’s investment accounts also made more than 20,000 trades last year.

entry.title: Jaylen Brown dissed Joel Embiid this offseason and now they're teammates
entry.content: The two stars better mend this fence, quickly.

entry.title: Jaylen Brown gets key contract update after stunning trade to 76ers
entry.content: Jaylen Brown has already received a key contract update after being traded to the Philadelphia 76ers from the Boston Celtics in the latest NBA blockbuster.

entry.title: Chris Brown to pay $13 million to housekeeper attacked by singer's Caucasian shepherd
entry.content: A jury found Chris Brown liable this week for the violent dog attack case involving his Caucasian shepherd and a housekeeper. He now owes nearly $13 million to the housekeeper.

entry.title: Apple is reportedly planning a visual refresh of the entry-level MacBook Pro next year
entry.content: Next spring could be a fiesta of new iPads, MacBooks and iPhones.

entry.title: Trump Made $1 Billion on Crypto Deals While His Fans Lost a Fortune
entry.content: Roughly two-thirds of investors in the president’s memecoin are currently in the red.

entry.title: Daredevils reach top of Empire State Building with banner, before apparent proposal
entry.content: Daredevils reach top of Empire State Building with banner, before apparent proposal

entry.title: US-UK drug deal could result in 229,000 excess deaths in England, analysis suggests
entry.content: Analysis reveals extent of impact on NHS of placating Donald Trump over price of British medicine exportsThe NHS will have to divert £45bn from essential services to pay for new medicines under the terms of the UK-US trade deal agreed last December, leading to more than 200,000 avoidable deaths of patients, analysis has found.Ministers have defended the deal as a way of helping British drug exports to the US avoid tariffs, and giving patients in England access to potentially life-extending drugs that would otherwise be denied. Continue reading...

entry.title: Commentary: Thank you, World Cup, for providing the connection and inspiration Americans so desperately need
entry.content: My family did not plan to make the World Cup the centerpiece of our summer, but once it began, we couldn't stop watching.

entry.title: Questing into her past with a camcorder, a young woman confronts the worst in 'Romería'
entry.content: Filmmaker Carla Simón completes a trilogy of personal movies that dive into her own history, her family's lingering grief and her journey to adulthood.

entry.title: ESPN is moving its L.A. operations to Hollywood Park in Inglewood
entry.content: The Disney-owned sports media company is exiting its downtown Los Angeles facility.

entry.title: Cash-strapped British military embarks on radical new course
entry.content: Following London's belated publication of its long-awaited, highly controversial Defense Investment Plan, political and defense circles in Britain are in an uproar, with the prime minister and the opposition leader sparring Wednesday in fiery exchanges in Parliament.

entry.title: Tobias Harris reportedly reaches 2-year, $31 million deal to join Spurs
entry.content: Tobias Harris helped lead the Pistons to the No. 1 seed in the Eastern Conference last season.

entry.title: New Jersey Devils re-sign captain Nico Hischier for 5 years, $58.5 million
entry.content: Talks with new general manager Sunny Mehta on the future led to Nico Hischier signing up to stay for the long term.

entry.title: Pistons free agency tracker: Live signings, trades, news, updates and more in 2026 NBA offseason
entry.content: This summer will be about building on a good thing for the Pistons.

entry.title: See where Miami Hurricanes rank in College Football 27 ratings
entry.content: With cover athlete Malachi Toney and Darian Mensah leading the way, the Miami Hurricanes rank among the best in the College Football 27 video game.

entry.title: Why Greg Gard, others like Wisconsin's choice of Shawn Eichorst as AD
entry.content: Greg Gard, Kelly Sheffield, Yvette Healy and Jim Delany are among the many coaches or administrators who have spoken highly of new AD Shawn Eichorst.

entry.title: Why Greg Gard, others like Wisconsin's choice of Shawn Eichorst as AD
entry.content: Greg Gard, Kelly Sheffield, Yvette Healy and Jim Delany are among the many coaches or administrators who have spoken highly of new AD Shawn Eichorst.

entry.title: Brother of former Jaguars' DL Calais Campbell charged in mother's death
entry.content: The brother of former Jaguars' DL Calais Campbell has been charged in mother's death.

entry.title: National Test Scores Are Dropping. This State Is Fighting Back.
entry.content: Schools across the U.S. are scrambling to help struggling readers. Tennessee’s back-to-basics approach is showing promise.

entry.title: Trump urges acting spy chief to declassify records, including on 2020 election
entry.content: Trump urges acting spy chief to declassify records, including on 2020 election

entry.title: John Brennan Sues Trump Administration Over Investigation Records
entry.content: The former CIA director argues that he needs the materials to challenge any potential indictment.

entry.title: How the Evolution of American Cities Defined a Nation
entry.content: The top U.S. cities exerted their influence as centers of commerce, trade, finance and dissent.

entry.title: U.S. Naval Research Laboratory is building 'decision superiority' tools with AI
entry.content: American warfighters equipped with the latest technology increasingly find themselves grappling with an endless sea of information -- so much so that the U.S. military needs new tools to cut through and make sense of the data overload.

entry.title: Changing the Constitution: Five Proposals for a More Perfect Union
entry.content: We asked five experts across the political spectrum: What would you change about the U.S. Constitution if you could?

entry.title: Lone star ticks are covering much of the U.S. Here's what you need to know
entry.content: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.

entry.title: Canada to ​join Eurovision song contest from 2027
entry.content: Country is first to join since Australia in 2015 as event director says it ‘continues to welcome the world’Canada will ​join the Eurovision song contest in 2027, becoming the first new ⁠participant since Australia in 2015, organisers have announced.Participation is not limited to countries in geographic Europe and instead is open to all members of the European Broadcasting Union (EBU), which Canada joined last week. Australia is an associate member. Continue reading...

entry.title: Lethal Weapon star Danny Glover reveals Alzheimer’s diagnosis
entry.content: The 79-year-old actor told media ‘I don’t feel like it’s the end of my life. There’s work to do. Your life continues’Lethal Weapon actor Danny Glover has revealed he has been living with Alzheimer’s for several years.Glover, 79, announced the news during an interview on The Today Show, during which he explained that he had been diagnosed “not long” after receiving an honorary Oscar in 2022. “I could live with it, in a sense. I’m sure as it advances, things are going to be different and changing,” he said, adding that his movements, speech and memory have slowed. However, the support of his family, who he said “have got my back”, was getting him through. Continue reading...

entry.title: ‘The silence was the worst part’: after culling his entire flock, US poultry farmer now fears bird flu
entry.content: As bird flu reshapes agriculture, small farms operate under strict rules. Just a single case could tank their businessJoshua Beebe often starts his day by cleaning the tires of trucks and cars entering his poultry farm.“We spray them off and scrub them with a brush. It’s a precaution; the goal is to eliminate as many potential avenues for a pathogen to enter as possible,” said the owner of Tardif Poultry Farm, located in the Connecticut countryside east of Hartford. Continue reading...

entry.title: Inflation peaked in May as energy prices fell in June, Kalshi traders think
entry.content: Speculators on the prediction market platform give less than 30% odds to inflation peaking above 4.2% in 2026.

entry.title: Author of England maternity care review ‘listened to wrong voices’, says adviser
entry.content: Dr Bill Kirkup says section of Valerie Amos’s report criticising ‘normal birth ideology’ was removed before publicationThe head of an inquiry into maternity care altered its final report to remove criticism of “normal birth ideology”, one of her expert advisers claims.Dr Bill Kirkup said Valerie Amos “listened to the wrong voices” before a section outlining the potential risks of encouraging women to have a vaginal birth “disappeared” from the final version of her government-commissioned report. Continue reading...

entry.title: British actor Michael Byrne dies at 82
entry.content: British actor Michael Byrne, known for his roles in “Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows: Part 1” and “Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade,” died June 20.

entry.title: What’s really in a hotdog? Nutrition experts explain
entry.content: Americans eat 20bn hotdogs every year, but experts say they’re also among the most highly processed foodsIn 1969, the late writer William Zinsser toured a hotdog factory and described his visit in Life magazine, opening with the lines: “I’ve often wondered what goes into a hotdog. Now I know and I wish I didn’t.”All these years later, his words still reflect our love/hate relationship with the humble wiener. We love eating them, but would rather not think about what’s in them. Continue reading...

entry.title: 'Magnificent 7' stocks' rough June pushes them into the red for the year. What's behind the decline
entry.content: The group — which Microsoft, Nvidia, Alphabet, Apple, Meta, Tesla and Amazon — shaved off roughly $2 trillion in market cap during June.

entry.title: Can creditors charge interest on unpaid debts after someone dies?
entry.content: When it comes to interest, what happens after death depends on the debt, the estate and the settlement timeline.

entry.title: Actor Danny Glover reveals Alzheimer's diagnosis
entry.content: Actor Danny Glover spoke about his Alzheimer's diagnosis in an interview with NBC's "Today" show, revealing that he has been living with the disease for several years.

entry.title: Trump lauds "very good meetings" as indirect U.S.-Iran talks resume
entry.content: Vance says the U.S. has accomplished its "core mission" in Iran, as Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner meet mediators in Qatar, but not Iranians.

entry.title: Sony to stop making physical discs for PlayStation starting in 2028
entry.content: After January 2028, new games will be available exclusively in digital format from the PlayStation Store and at retailers, Sony said.

entry.title: USMNT "ready to run through a wall" ahead of World Cup knockout stage, Charlie Davies says
entry.content: The U.S. men's national soccer team performed well in the group stage of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, but they haven't won a knockout match since 2002. "It's the pressure time," former USMNT player Charlie Davies said.

entry.title: Venezuelan police officers arrested over alleged looting after earthquakes
entry.content: Anger at authorities and government grows as local people, volunteers and rescue teams continue search for survivors Four Venezuelan police officers have been arrested and are facing dismissal after being accused of looting cash from the rubble of a building that collapsed during last week’s devastating twin earthquakes.Local people and national and international rescue teams continue to search for survivors in the aftermath of the back-to-back quakes, which have killed almost 2,000 people, injured more than 10,000, and left tens of thousands missing. Continue reading...
